headline: 3 Food Stocks With Big Yields. Why Wells Fargo Says Be Careful.
summary: Wells Fargo downgraded Conagra Brands, Campbell’s, and General Mills, warning inflation and sluggish consumption could pressure earnings even as their dividend yields top 6%.

headline: Wells Fargo downgrades Conagra, Campbell’s and General Mills on profit risks
summary: Investing.com -- Wells Fargo has downgraded shares of Conagra Brands, The Campbell's Company and General Mills to Underweight in a note on Thursday, warning that a combination of earnings pressure, elevated leverage and stretched dividend payout ratios leaves the group vulnerable to underperformance.

headline: Here Are Thursday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: Arista Networks, Crocs, General Mills, GoDaddy, Jabil, Lumentum, MercadoLibre, Shopify, and More
summary: Pre-Market Stock Futures: Futures are trading lower this morning, as issues in the Strait of Hormuz get murkier. headline: Why Investors Aren’t Fleeing to Safe-Haven Stocks
summary: When investors fret over geopolitical shocks, they usually rotate into safe havens such as consumer staples and healthcare.  Less obviously, tech and software stocks have also advanced, while traditional safety plays such as healthcare and packaged food have sold off.  By the time hostilities began, those defensive sectors were already crowded—and no longer quite as cheap.

headline: Wells Fargo Downgrades General Mills to Underweight, Lowers Price Target to $35
summary: Wells Fargo  analyst Chris Carey   downgrades General Mills (NYSE:GIS) from Equal-Weight to Underweight and lowers the price target from $45 to $35.
